connections.name: Meta Air Link
requirements: Router in same room as play space

connections.name: Virtual Desktop
requirements: Virtual Desktop app purchased on Quest Store

connections.name: Steam Link VR
requirements: Steam Link app on headset (free on Horizon Store)

knownIssues.title: LCD panel has lower contrast than OLED predecessors
knownIssues.description: The Quest 3S uses an LCD panel, resulting in grey blacks instead of true black. Dark VR environments will appear washed out compared to OLED headsets.
knownIssues.workaround: Increase in-game brightness in dark scenes. Not fixable in software — a hardware limitation of LCD technology.

knownIssues.title: Quest Link shader compilation stutters on first launch
knownIssues.description: First-time use of Quest Link causes shader compilation stutters in SteamVR games. Resolves after initial caching.
knownIssues.workaround: Let SteamVR finish shader pre-caching on first launch. Avoid clearing shader cache unnecessarily.

knownIssues.title: Air Link bitrate drops on congested Wi-Fi
knownIssues.description: Air Link automatically reduces bitrate when Wi-Fi congestion is detected, causing visible compression artifacts.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a dedicated 5GHz SSID for the Quest. Move router closer to play space. Consider a Wi-Fi 6E router.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Oculus OpenXR runtime for native Quest games
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Using Meta's own VR software makes games run smoother because it cuts out the SteamVR middleman.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Oculus OpenXR runtime has lower overhead than the SteamVR runtime for Quest Link/Air Link connections. Set Oculus as the active OpenXR runtime in Oculus PC App settings for 5-15% frame time improvement in OpenXR titles.

optimizationTips.tip: The Quest 3S lower resolution makes supersampling more impactful
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turning up the picture quality setting makes a bigger difference on the Quest 3S than on higher-resolution headsets.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: At 1832x1920 per eye, the Quest 3S has a lower pixel density than the Quest 3. Supersampling at 1.2-1.3x in the Oculus app significantly sharpens aliased edges. This is more cost-effective than on the Quest 3 due to the lower base resolution.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Wi-Fi 6 and use 5GHz band for wireless streaming
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Make sure your router uses the fastest Wi-Fi band available to reduce motion-to-photon latency.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) on 5GHz provides up to 1.2Gbps throughput with lower latency than Wi-Fi 5. Use a dedicated SSID on a non-DFS 5GHz channel (36, 40, 44, 48). Set channel width to 80MHz minimum for stable streaming at 120Hz.
